judgment.summary: A profitable company with strong returns and free cash flow, but facing revenue headwinds and high leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 0.7%; next earnings date is 2026-08-18.
positives: Free cash flow $10.13B TTM — substantial cash generation.
Operating margin 11.9% and Profit margin 8.4% — healthy profitability.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $372.85.
negatives: Revenue growth 4.8% YoY and analyst growth estimates of 1.9% FY — modest growth.
Quarterly earnings growth -4.2% YoY — recent earnings decline.

news.paragraph: Home Depot's peer Lowe's reported increased sales driven by professional business growth, while Walmart flagged rising fuel costs impacting earnings. Although these headlines provide sector context, they do not directly address Home Depot's immediate performance or upcoming catalysts. The next earnings date is August 18th, leaving a period of observation for any specific company news.

headlines.title: Walmart Flags Higher Fuel Costs Eroding Retailer’s Earnings
Lowe’s Sales Rise, Boosted by Pro Business Growth
Analyst Report: The Home Depot, Inc.

examples.body: $QXO ,$HD, $GS, $AMD, 23% short position in QXO, looking for strong reversal . They are participating in data center building .
$SPY Stagflation incoming. Home Depot is the canary in the coal mine. $HD
$SPY silver and gold- Wait till after next ER to buy $WMT and $HD. It’s going lower.
$HD $WMT Home Depot’s PE is half Walmart. Nice try I’m sure if Walmart PE was 20 it would be up today. Instead it’s 44
